component.name: Banner
component.description: A prominent, full-width message strip — typically pinned to the top of a view or section — for system-level information or errors that affect the whole page.
component.note: 12px padding, sharp corners, message wraps. error = danger.700 fill + white text + warning icon (role=alert); info = neutral.50 fill + neutral.800 text + info icon (role=status). Synced to Figma '❖ Banner'.

appearances.error: bg colors.background.danger.button (danger.700, AA-safe with white); text colors.text.neutral.inverse (white); warning icon (white, 24px)
appearances.info: bg colors.background.neutral.weak (neutral.50); text colors.text.neutral.strong (neutral.800); info icon (neutral.800, 24px)

useCases: Page- or app-level error that blocks or affects the whole view
A persistent message that belongs at the top of a region

commonPatterns.name: Top-of-page error
commonPatterns.description: Pinned above page content; assertive so screen readers announce it.
commonPatterns.jsx: <Banner appearance='error'>We couldn’t save your changes. Try again.</Banner>

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a banner for transient, low-importance feedback
antiPatterns.reason: Banners are prominent and page-level; minor confirmations are better as lighter, transient UI.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use an inline message or a (future) toast.

antiPatterns.reason: A page-level banner is too heavy and detached from the field.
Competing prominent strips overwhelm the page and bury the priority message.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using info styling for a blocking error
antiPatterns.reason: Low-emphasis neutral styling under-communicates severity and isn't announced assertively.

slots.icon: Auto warning icon for error, info icon for info; override or set null

interactions.static: Non-interactive message; the Figma version has no actions or dismiss
interactions.wrapping: Long text wraps to multiple lines

accessibility.role: error → alert (assertive); info → status (polite); overridable via role
accessibility.screenReader: Announced on render per role; the icon is aria-hidden
accessibility.contrastNote: error uses danger.700 (white text 5.81:1, AA); info uses neutral.800 on neutral.50.

figmaDivergences.reason: 4px crowds the 24px icon against the text; 8px reads better and matches the rendered spacing.
Real banners contain full messages; truncating system errors would hide critical detail.

aiHints.context: Reach for Banner for page- or app-level messages — system errors, maintenance, account notices — pinned to the top of a view. Use error for blocking problems (assertive), info for neutral notices. For field errors use inline messages; for transient confirmation prefer lighter UI.
neverUseWhen: The feedback is transient/minor — use a lighter inline message or toast
It's a single form field error — use inline field error text
Severity is high but you reach for info — use error
You need several at once — show only the top-priority banner
